title: The word that unlocked a private repo
summary: Security researchers at Noma Security tricked GitHub's new AI coding agent into leaking a private repository's contents to a public issue — an indirect prompt injection where the word 'Additionally' slipped past the guardrails. And under the noise of Google's big I/O keynote, the one thing you can actually download today: Gemma 4, an Apache-2 open-weight family whose 12B model drops the separate vision and audio encoders. Plus a sweep sizing I/O honestly, SambaNova's raise, Microsoft's in-house models, and Alberta's government code audit.

topics: GitLost: indirect prompt injection leaks private repos via GitHub Agentic Workflows
Gemma 4 open-weight family (encoder-free native multimodal, 5:1 local:global attention)

dives.story: GitLost — GitHub AI agent tricked into leaking a private repo
dives.why_it_matters: Anyone wiring an LLM agent to untrusted inputs (issues, tickets, emails) with broad permissions has built a confused deputy; the fix is least privilege, not a better guardrail.
Developers who need a model they can download, run, and fine-tune get a capable Apache-2 multimodal family engineered to be memory-cheap — the substance of Google's day versus the keynote's roadmap.

concepts_taught: the confused-deputy problem (prompt injection as its descendant)
encoder-free native multimodal models (dropping separate vision/audio encoders)
